books.title: Gil Blas
books.author: Alain-René Lesage
books.summary: A poor youth encounters picaresque adventures as he travels through 17th century Spain.

books.title: The Purple Land
books.author: W. H. Hudson
books.summary: An Englishman becomes embroiled in adventure, politics, and romance in 19th-century Uruguay.

books.title: Personal Memoirs of Ulysses S. Grant
books.author: Ulysses S. Grant
books.summary: One of the greatest generals of the United States tells the story of his life and details his key role in winning the Civil War.

books.title: A General History of the Pirates
books.author: Captain Charles Johnson
books.summary: The lives and stories of the daring pirate outlaws who terrorized the Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea in the 18th century.

books.title: The Secret of the Caves
books.author: Franklin W. Dixon
books.summary: Four boys vacation at nearby caves only to be swept up in a mystery.

books.title: The Surprising Adventures of Baron Munchausen
books.author: Rudolph Erich Raspe
books.summary: The legendary Baron Munchausen’s tall tales of wonder and adventure.

books.title: Ajax
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: A great warrior descends into madness after being denied magical armor.

books.title: The Master Mind of Mars
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: A new hero from Earth reaches Mars and battles a merchant of purloined youth.

books.title: El Dorado
books.author: Baroness Orczy
books.summary: Two separate interests want to free the imprisoned Dauphin of France.

books.title: Ghost Stories
books.author: E. F. Benson
books.summary: A collection of ghost stories by E. F. Benson.

books.title: Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl
books.author: Linda Brent
books.summary: The autobiography of a woman who grew up in, and escaped from, slavery.

books.title: The D’Arblay Mystery
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A doctor finds himself entangled in the mysterious death of an artist after discovering his body in a secluded pond.

books.title: The Worst Journey in the World
books.author: Apsley Cherry-Garrard
books.summary: The travelogue of a member of Robert Scott’s ill-fated Antarctic expedition of 1910.

books.title: The Crock of Gold
books.author: James Stephens
books.summary: A philosopher sets out to rescue the beautiful Caitilin Ni Murrachu from the god Pan in an adventure that involves leprechauns, Celtic gods, policemen, and philosophical disquisitions both profound and absurd.

books.title: Metamorphoses
books.author: Ovid
books.summary: A collection of Greek and Roman myths that involve various forms of transformation.

books.title: Vile Bodies
books.author: Evelyn Waugh
books.summary: A young man in Roaring Twenties London endures ups and downs in varied situations and relationships.

books.title: The Island of Doctor Moreau
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A shipwrecked man is rescued by a boat and left on a strange island owned by a physiologist who experiments in vivisection.

books.title: Jeeves Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of short stories featuring Jeeves and Wooster and the upperclass English life of the early 1900s.

books.title: The Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.summary: Dante journeys through Hell, Purgatory, and Heaven in order to receive salvation and to find divine love.

books.title: Journals
books.author: Alexander Mackenzie
books.summary: Explorer Alexander Mackenzie details his journeys to both the Arctic and Pacific Oceans in his personal journals.
